CP00-806 is a sound descriptor-only pack. Key checks that passed under read-only inspection:
- **Coverage** recomputed from the live plan matches the binding/requirements exactly (10 units; test 2 / security_audit 2 / implementation 6; RP26.P06=10).
- **No-write/no-real-data boundaries** enforced everywhere — `NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION` spread into every result, all case-set rows descriptor-only with payload flags false, validators + tests actively reject runtime/permission/audit/UI execution and tenant/secret leakage.
- **Authority boundary correct** — Claude read-only and non-final; human approval required for runtime; Hermes packets descriptor-only with no product runtime.
- **Contract** promotes CP00-806 as latest with CP805/prior historical and `validation.valid=true`.

The single **P3 (non-blocking)** is the standard pre-closeout transparency caveat: the dynamic validation runs and downstream artifacts (command-evidence, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are produced by/after this read-only review and couldn't be executed/observed here — so the `production_ready` flip must wait for those green runs. I recomputed coverage from the live plan (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json) included_units and it matches the binding and requirements exactly: 10 units; deliverable distribution test 2 / security_audit 2 / implementation 6; phase RP26.P06=10; micro RP26.P06.M02=2 and RP26.P06.M03=8; titles Type And Shape Definition 2 / Primary Implementation Slice 8. Registry row constants match the plan unit titles per micro-phase (PERMISSION_MATRIX_TEST_ROWS for M02; PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS.slice(0,8) for M03). Boundaries hold throughout: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is spread into every freezeResult; every case-set row is descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false with raw/tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payload flags false, and ROW_EXTRAS only add further false/descriptor-only flags; validators.js actively rejects runtime permission evaluation, permission-decision/audit writes, UI/API runtime, real tenant data, cross-tenant routes, key/secret material, and enterprise-trust claims; model.test.js asserts coverage/descriptor/contract validation errors===[] and keeps the M02 cross-tenant/leak-prevention test rows and the 8 M03 implementation rows runtime-closed. SSO/SCIM descriptors are descriptor-only with all runtime/exposure flags false and validated. The contract promotes CP00-806 to latest_pack and latest_projection (EnterpriseSaasCp806PermissionMatrixPrimaryImplementationDescriptor) with CP805 and prior packs historical and validation.valid=true; the validate script targets CP806 and falls back to the manifest plan_binding_snapshot. No secrets/PII observed in source or the generated contract.
